  "text": "The controller work queue with rate limiting is implemented primarily in k8s.io/client-go under util/workqueue/. The core interface files are:\n\n1. **RateLimitingInterface** is defined in `util/workqueue/rate_limiting_queue.go` (sg-evals/client-go--v0.32.0) and its mirror `staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/rate_limiting_queue.go` (sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0). It extends TypedDelayingInterface and adds AddRateLimited/Forget/NumRequeues methods.\n\n2. **ItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter** and **BucketRateLimiter** are both defined in `util/workqueue/default_rate_limiters.go` (client-go) and its staging mirror. ItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter implements baseDelay*2^<num-failures> backoff. BucketRateLimiter adapts golang.org/x/time/rate.Limiter to the RateLimiter API. Both have typed variants: TypedItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter[T] and TypedBucketRateLimiter[T].\n\n3. The controller **reconcile loop** pattern using queue.Add/queue.Get/queue.Done is illustrated in `examples/workqueue/main.go` (client-go) showing the canonical Controller struct with indexer, queue, and informer. The processNextItem function calls queue.Get(), defers queue.Done(key), and calls queue.AddRateLimited on failure.\n\n4. Many Kubernetes controllers use this pattern: pkg/controller/certificates/certificate_controller.go, pkg/controller/namespace/namespace_controller.go (also shows TypedBucketRateLimiter and TypedItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter usage), pkg/controller/bootstrap/tokencleaner.go, pkg/controller/volume/pvprotection/pv_protection_controller.go, pkg/controller/serviceaccount/serviceaccounts_controller.go, pkg/controller/clusterroleaggregation/clusterroleaggregation_controller.go, and apiextensions/apiserver controllers in staging/."

  "text": "The controller work queue with rate limiting is implemented primarily in k8s.io/client-go under util/workqueue/. The definitions exist both in the standalone client-go repo and the staging mirror in kubernetes.\n\n**RateLimitingInterface** is defined in `util/workqueue/rate_limiting_queue.go` (client-go) and its mirror `staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/rate_limiting_queue.go` (kubernetes). It is a deprecated alias for TypedRateLimitingInterface[any]. TypedRateLimitingInterface[T] extends TypedDelayingInterface[T] and adds AddRateLimited/Forget/NumRequeues methods.\n\n**ItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter** is in `util/workqueue/default_rate_limiters.go` — a deprecated alias for TypedItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter[any]. It implements baseDelay*2^<num-failures> exponential backoff per item.\n\n**BucketRateLimiter** is also in `util/workqueue/default_rate_limiters.go` — a deprecated alias for TypedBucketRateLimiter[any]. It wraps golang.org/x/time/rate.Limiter to adapt a token bucket to the workqueue RateLimiter API.\n\nThe **controller reconcile loop** pattern using queue.Add/queue.Get/queue.Done is illustrated in `examples/workqueue/main.go` (both repos). The canonical pattern has a processNextWorkItem function that calls queue.Get() to receive an item, defers queue.Done(key), processes the item, and calls queue.AddRateLimited(key) on error with queue.Forget(key) on success.\n\nNumerous Kubernetes controllers implement this pattern: certificate_controller.go uses explicit TypedBucketRateLimiter and TypedItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter composition; namespace_controller.go defines its own nsControllerRateLimiter combining both; other controllers (tokencleaner, pvprotection, serviceaccounts, clusterroleaggregation, etc.) use DefaultTypedControllerRateLimiter which combines both under the hood."
}
